title: Infinite URL Spaces, Calendars, Facets, Sessions, and Budget Defense · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A disability-led public-interest technology team incrementally builds URL-space trap classifier and containment policy from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, browser, and report fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of URL-space trap classifier and containment policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through predictio…
objectives: Detect calendar, pagination, faceting, sorting, search, session, nonce, path-depth, repeated-segment, and parameter-combination expansion.
Budget distinct keys, values, combinations, orderings, and per-template pages with stakeholder-approved exceptions.
Classify soft 404, login, challenge, empty shell, redirect loop, and repeated-template responses using multiple signals.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Trap Patterns
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 9849 during an isolated browser, package, and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to contain calendar, facet, session, template, soft-error, and query-cardinality traps with stop evidence; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then change one test, mutation, package, browser binary, container, capacity, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Detect calendar, pagination, faceting, sorting, search, session, nonce, path-depth, repeated-segment, and parameter-combination expansion.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Trap Patterns.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for detect calendar, pagination, faceting, sorting, search, session, nonce, path-depth, repeated-segment, and parameter-combination expansion. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Trap Patterns, keep Trap Patterns connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

options.text: State the Trap Patterns preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
Duplicate URL strings are the only cause of infinite crawls. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.

For commit to the mission model, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof.
